reasoning_details.text:
We need to translate the given chunk from English to Hebrew, following the detailed instructions. The chunk is a full article with YAML frontmatter, MDX components, code blocks, and prose. We must preserve all code, MDX tags, markdown structure, and relative URLs (prepend ../). Translate only reader-facing text. For YAML, translate title, subTitle, and any other reader-facing values. Set language to "Hebrew". Omit date, draft, popularity, etc. Keep modified. Preserve tags, category, social_image, cover paths (with ../). Also note the blockquote and footnote. Let's go step by step. First, YAML frontmatter: - title: "It's Time for llm:// Connection Strings" -> translate to Hebrew. - subTitle: "Simplify Model & Provider Config with `llm://` URLs" -> translate. - date: omit (inherited metadata). - modified: keep as is. - tags: keep as is (list of English tags? The instruction says preserve taxonomy metadata exactly, so keep tags as is? But tags are reader-facing? Usually tags are not translated in technical articles. The instruction says "Preserve taxonomy and routing metadata exactly: category, subCategory, tags, related, redirects, commentsKeyOverride, label, modified, and minReleaseDate." So keep tags as is. But the example in the contract says "Translate only reader-facing frontmatter values: language, title, subTitle, cover_alt, and cover_credit." So tags are not reader-facing? Actually tags are often visible to readers, but the instruction explicitly lists what to translate. So we keep tags as is. - category: keep as is. - draft: omit. - popularity: omit. - social_image: keep filename, but prepend ../? The instruction says "ALL relative URL paths for images, CSS, links, or other resources should prefix/prepend with a `../`." So social_image is a relative path? It's just a filename, no directory. But it's in the same folder? The original has no path, just filename. In the translated file, we need to prepend ../? The instruction says "for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders". Since this is a translation into a locale folder (e.g., he/), the assets are in the parent folder. So we should prepend ../ to social_image, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon. So social_image: ../desktop-social.webp? But the original is just desktop-social.webp. The instruction says "preserve social_image, cover, cover_full_width, cover_mobile, and cover_icon filenames, but use ../ for inherited local asset paths inside locale folders." So we change to ../desktop-social.webp. Similarly for cover_full_width: ../hero-wide.webp, cover_mobile: ../square-200.webp, cover_icon: ../square-200.webp. - cover_full_width, cover_mobile, cover_icon: same. - Also note: the original has cover_full_width: ./hero-wide.webp with ./ prefix. We should change to ../hero-wide.webp (remove ./ and add ../). The instruction says "prefix/prepend with a ../". So we replace ./ with ../. Now the body: - Blockquote with update: translate the text inside. Keep the
tag. Translate "Update: This article led to an [Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-levy-llm-uri-scheme/)." The link URL stays, translate link text? The link text is "Internet-Draft for the `llm://` URI scheme" - translate that. Also the inline code `llm://` stays. - Then prose: "Remember the bad old days..." translate to Hebrew. Keep the tone direct, technical, with humor. Use native idioms where appropriate. For example, "tower of delicate config" -> maybe "מגדל קלפים של קונפיגורציה". "fragile house of cards" -> "בית קלפים רעוע". "where the hell does the timeout setting go?" -> "ואיפה לעזאזל שמים את ה-timeout?" Keep the code block with postgres URL unchanged. - Then "So why are we treating LLMs like it's 1999?" -> translate. - Heading "## The Env Var Explosion" -> translate. - Prose: "Right now, my `.env` file looks like a graveyard..." translate. Keep inline code `.env`, `OPENAI_API_KEY`, etc. Translate the rest. - "It's not just ugly; it's friction." -> translate. - "What if we just... ~~stole~~ borrowed the DB URL idea?" -> translate, keep strikethrough. - Heading "## Introducing LLM Connection Strings" -> translate. - Prose: "Imagine configuring..." translate. Keep code block with llm:// URLs unchanged. - Then "---" and "
" - preserve. - Heading "### Anatomy of an LLM Connection String" -> translate. - Image: ![the parts of a LLM connection string](./inline-url-diagram-dark.svg) -> translate alt text to Hebrew, and change URL to ../inline-url-diagram-dark.svg. - Prose: "The scheme is `llm://`..." translate. - Heading "## Need auth? Great, add it." -> translate. - Prose: "Just like `postgres://`..." translate. Keep code block. - Note block: translate the note text. Keep inline code. - Heading "## Resiliency? Why the hell not." -> translate. - Prose: "Many database libraries..." translate. Keep code block. - Blockquote: "One string with everything..." translate. - Heading "## Alternative Formats" -> translate. - Prose: "I'm not married to `llm://`..." translate. - Code block with ollama, vercel, bedrock URLs - keep unchanged. - List: translate the three points. - Blockquote: "The database world took decades..." translate. Keep tags. - Heading "## The Verdict" -> translate. - Prose: "We don't need another..." translate. - Image: ![a messy env var drawer](./hero-concept-8-drawers.webp) -> translate alt text, change URL to ../hero-concept-8-drawers.webp. - Comment: {/* ¹ Yes, I know... */} - translate the comment text? The instruction says "Preserve ALL inline code spans (`...`)." and "Preserve ALL MDX component tags and their prop names." Comments are not MDX components, but they are JSX comments. Should we translate the text inside? The instruction says "Translate only reader-facing prose." Comments are not reader-facing (they are not rendered). So we should keep the comment as is? But the comment is in English and is a joke. The instruction says "Translate figurative English by intent, not by word-for-word mimicry." But since it's a comment, it's not visible to readers. However, the instruction also says "Output ONLY the translated text." and "Preserve ALL code blocks exactly." Comments are not code blocks. I think we should translate the comment text as well, because it's part of the content that might be seen in source. But the instruction says "reader-facing". I'll translate it to Hebrew to maintain consistency, but keep the JSX comment syntax. The footnote reference ¹ is in the prose earlier. We need to translate the footnote text as well. The footnote is at the bottom in a comment. Translate it. Also note: The original has a footnote marker ¹ in the prose: "Then, someone had the brilliant idea to just use a URL¹:" - we need to keep that superscript? It's just a number. We'll keep it as is. The footnote text is in the comment at the end. Now, let's produce the translation. We'll write in Hebrew, using appropriate technical terms. For example, "connection strings" -> "מחרוזות חיבור". "env var explosion" -> "פיצוץ משתני סביבה". "resiliency" -> "עמידות". "failover" -> "גיבוי". "hyperparameters" -> "היפר-פרמטרים". "portability" -> "ניידות". "CLI friendly" -> "ידידותי לשורת הפקודה". "language agnostic" -> "בלתי תלוי בשפה". We need to preserve the exact markdown heading levels. The article has H2 and H3 headings. Keep them. Also note the blockquote with class "inset" and "ai-response". Keep those classes. Now, write the translation.
